title: Christmasbusters
intro: It's Christmas. Karl's bought everyone the same present. He won't say what it is. Three clues, all Christmas-themed.
prize: Email in. Winner gets a copy of Shrek on DVD and a keyring from a service station.

clues.clue: There's a mint — like the place they make coins — and it's gone all crumbly. Like a pie. A little crumbly pie.
This woman called Miss Ellie — she's got toes. Not unusual. But she's gone and lost her toes somewhere. They've just gone.
There's a fairy, right, and she's feeling a bit light-headed. A bit dizzy. She's not well.

title: Christmasbusters
intro: It's Christmas. Karl's bought everyone the same present. Three clues, all Christmas-themed.
prize: Email in. Winner gets an AOL trial disc and a travel mug from a company that no longer exists.

It's really quiet, yeah, but also there's a knight. A knight in armour. Very quiet knight. Not making a sound.
Someone called Jim — or Jingle, but let's say Jim — he's gone and bought some bells. Just bells. Loads of them.

title: Christmasbusters
intro: It's Christmas. Karl's bought everyone the same present. He says it's practical. Three clues, all Christmas-themed.

This man called Tim, right, he's got himself all tangled up in something sparkly. Thin sparkly stuff. Wrapped right round him.
This fella named Advent, he's gone and added a calendar to his wall. Just put it up. Stuck it there. Every day there's a little door.

title: Halloweenbusters

There's a wolf, right, and he's gone a bit furry. Even more than usual. And he's howling at a big round moon. Bit much.
There's a big black cat, and she's gone and brewed something. In a big pot. Stirring it. Looks terrible. Bit bubbly.

title: Halloweenbusters
intro: Halloween. Karl saw a ghost. He says it knocked his bin over. Three clues, all spooky.
prize: Email in. Winner gets an AOL trial disc and a glow-in-the-dark rubber spider.

Someone's haunted a house. Just gone in there and haunted it. And now the house is a bit rattly.
There's a big orange vegetable and someone's had a good carve at it. Done a face on it. Bit threatening the face.

title: Halloweenbusters
intro: Halloween. Karl says he's not scared of anything except those hands that come up out of the ground. Three clues.
prize: Email in. Winner gets a DVD of The Sixth Sense and a free keyring from a petrol station forecourt.

There's a fella called Nos, and he's gone a bit ratty. Like a rat. Bit pointed in the face.
There's a coffin, right, and someone's left the lid on. Just left it. It's got a lid. Most things have got lids but not usually coffins.

There's a bunny, and someone's gone and covered it in chocolate. Top to bottom. All chocolatey. The bunny's not best pleased.

Someone had a supper. The last one they were going to have. They made a meal of it. Literally, they made a meal of it.

This fella called Len, right, and he's gone and lent something. Not for long though. Forty days. He'll want it back.

It's the season, right, and lambs are being born. Just lambing about everywhere. Tiny lambs. Very lamb-intensive period.

title: Easterbusters
intro: Easter. Karl says he once knew a bloke called Laz. Lovely fella. Had a bit of a lying-about problem. Three clues.

clues.clue: There's this fella, right, and he's had a good laze. A proper lie-about. And then gone a bit russ — gone rusty, like. Just all rusty from the lazing. You'd say it: laze-a-russ.
There's an alley, right — a back alley — and someone's nipped in there to use the loo. Very casual about it. Done a number one. You'd say it out loud: alley-loo-ya. Alleluia.
There's a king, right, and he lives in a wall. A wall-king. And he's decided to go for a stroll. On the water. Not in a boat — on it. Wall-king on water.

title: Easterbusters

There's a school, right, and someone's walked past it. Didn't go in. Just passed it. Pass-school. And there's a candle burning in the window. Pass-school-candle. Paschal Candle.
There's five thousand people, right, and someone's fed them all. From a packed lunch. Two fish and a few loaves. Five thousand. Karl says he'd want to know the logistics of that.

There's a pen, right, and the cost of it was outrageous. Just a pen. Pen-tuh-cost. Karl says he got one from a hotel once and that was basically free. Pentecost.

There's a massive great log, and someone keeps saying 'you'll be alright' to it. Just reassuring the log. Talking to it. The log seems fine but they keep saying it anyway.

title: Christmasbusters

There are three fellas — three of them — and they're all very wise. Properly wise, not just a bit. And they've come an enormous distance on camels to give presents to a baby they've never met. Bit forward really.

title: Christmasbusters
intro: It's Christmas. Karl's read the instructions on the turkey and he's not happy about how long it takes. Three clues, all Christmas-themed.

There's a massive great sled — horses pulling it, the full setup — and it's absolutely covered in bells. Loads of bells. It's going down the road just belling everything and everyone.

title: Christmasbusters

Someone's gone 'Oh!' at a very religious knight. Holy armour, the whole thing. And they've walked into him in the dark. Just 'Oh!' at a holy knight in the corridor at night.
There's a lad called Nat, and he's on a drip — hospital thing, an IV — and he's gone all itty-bitty and dramatic about it. Nat-iv-itty. And then his whole class does a play about what happened.

title: Christmasbusters
intro: It's Christmas. Karl's wrapped all his presents in newspaper because he ran out of paper. He says it adds character. Three clues, all Christmas-themed.
prize: Email in. Winner gets a Blockbuster loyalty card and a Now 52 cassette.

clues.clue: There's a fella called Dec — you know, off the telly — and he's gone and thumped someone in the corridors. Just absolutely decked them. In the halls. Dec's gone and decked a bloke in the halls.
There are some nuts — chest nuts, apparently from someone's chest area — and they've been stuck on a fire. They're roasting away. The chest nuts are having a really good roast.
There's a woman called Carol, right, and she's brought her mate Ling. Carol and Ling. They've both turned up at the front door at half ten at night and started singing. Bit much.

title: Christmasbusters
intro: It's Christmas. Karl keeps finding tinsel in his food and he's not sure how it's getting there. Three clues, all Christmas-themed.
prize: Email in. Winner gets a printed photo from a disposable camera, framed.

clues.clue: There's a fella called Chris — lot of Chrises at this time of year — and he's massed all his stockings together. Big pile of them. Chris's mass stocking situation. All hung up by the chimney.
There's a glass of brandy — lovely drop — and someone's gone and mixed butter into it. Just stirred butter straight into the brandy. That's not normal behaviour. Brandy and butter, combined.
There are twelve fellas, and they've all turned up at night. Just appeared, twelve of them, in the dark outside. Twelfth night. Someone needs to tell them Christmas is over and it's time to go home.
